title: The Code Book
author: Simon Singh
content: “The history of human interaction is a constant battle between those who want to keep information secret and those who want to expose it.”
action: Record: Security is not a state, but an ongoing, high-stakes arms race; assume any secret is eventually discoverable.

title: Shannon's Information Theory
author: Claude Shannon
content: “The fundamental problem of communication is that of reproducing at one point either exactly or approximately a message selected at another point.”
action: Record: Reduce all operational data to its simplest signal to prevent noise from corrupting the message.

title: The RSA Algorithm
author: Rivest, Shamir, & Adleman
content: “Public-key cryptography enables secure communication without the need for a pre-shared secret key.”
action: Record: Leverage asymmetric structures to operate safely in untrusted environments; decouple identity from trust.

title: The Bitcoin Whitepaper
author: Satoshi Nakamoto
content: “A purely peer-to-peer version of electronic cash would allow online payments to be sent directly from one party to another without going through a financial institution.”
action: Record: Trust is a vulnerability; build systems that rely on cryptographic verification rather than centralized gatekeepers.

title: Gödel's Incompleteness Theorems
author: Kurt Gödel
content: “In any consistent formal system, there are statements that cannot be proven or disproven within that system.”
action: Record: Acknowledge that all logical systems have an inherent limit; do not rely on a single framework for absolute truth.
